# Questions in Livestream 155

Live Stream #155: The Tyranny of Hypocrisy

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:56</a> | Why is biblical ritual material so repetitive instead of more philosophical? | He says passages like Numbers 29 reflect Bronze Age ritual-calendar genre rather than later philosophical writing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=950" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:50</a> | Why was the exodus pharaoh not subjected to damnatio memoriae? | He says not every pharaoh received that treatment, and Ramesses II's long reign and succession context gave no obvious reason for it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=1142" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:02</a> | Did Akhenaten's damnatio memoriae begin under Tutankhamun or later? | He says the exact starting point is uncertain and may belong more to the later cleanup under his successors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=1231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:31</a> | What does Hagar's name mean in Egyptian? | He says the name may preserve an Egyptian form that was slightly adjusted in Israelite transmission, but the exact sense is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=1406" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:26</a> | In Judges 1, were places like Beth-shean and Megiddo still Egyptian garrison towns rather than simply Canaanite towns? | He says they were Egyptian-controlled garrison centers, but the biblical wording can still function as a geographic label within Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=1553" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:53</a> | Why does God tell Balaam to go and then become angry when he goes? | He says Balaam keeps pressing for permission, receives a constrained allowance, and then goes in a disobedient spirit anyway. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2035" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:55</a> | What are his thoughts on Alex O'Connor, Cosmic Skeptic? | He says he has watched little of O'Connor, finds him generally reasonable but not especially interesting or insightful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2163" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:03</a> | What is his position on the philosophy of language? | He says he has no developed position because his philosophy work focused elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:36</a> | Why is he a Christian? | He answers "grace," then explains that hardship and crises of faith repeatedly drove him back to God and evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:48</a> | Why do people pray? | He says prayer aligns a person's mind with God's thoughts and helps Christians endure hardship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:29</a> | What are his thoughts on the transcendental argument for God's existence? | He says he is not familiar enough with the argument to comment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=2898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:18</a> | What is the problem with atheism? | He says atheism poorly accounts for phenomenology and has pragmatic problems, including lack of theistic support structures and forgiveness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=3643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:43</a> | Why did Tutankhamun's widow ask the Hittites for a prince to marry? | He says her local options were bad and the request makes sense as a desperate political move. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=4599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:39</a> | How do henotheism, monolatry, and monotheism differ? | He says henotheism is a kind of monolatry, while monotheism denies the existence of other gods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=7994" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:14</a> | Are there extra-Israelite accounts of the conquest? | He says there are not direct conquest accounts comparable to Joshua, but there is relevant external and archaeological context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=8990" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:50</a> | What are his thoughts on Christians becoming politicians? | He says it can be preferable or not depending on ethics, calling, and circumstances rather than a simple rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=9333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:33</a> | Did Jethro advise Moses before or after the giving of the Ten Commandments? | He says the chronology depends on reading Exodus's arrangement carefully and should not be flattened too quickly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=9818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:38</a> | Did Abraham leave Haran after Terah died? | He says Acts 7 reads Genesis 11-12 as Abraham leaving after Terah's death, based on the consecutive-vav sequence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=11089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:49</a> | Is David McClellan worth reading if his theory is rejected? | He says McClellan can be useful on Hebrew and Iron Age material, but not on Egypt-related claims. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=11162" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:02</a> | Should the church address racial inequality and climate change to revive? | He says creation care matters, but the church's central focus must remain the gospel rather than climate or social-justice priorities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=11844" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:24</a> | Were there Egyptian coastal forts east or northeast of Migdol on the Way of Horus, and did they have chariots? | He says yes, there were forts beyond Migdol, and the major forts on the route would have had chariots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=12325" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:25</a> | What books are best for learning about ancient Egypt's relationship to and influence on the Bible? | He recommends James Hoffmeier's work, especially books on Israel in Egypt and Israel in Sinai. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=12539" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:59</a> | How does he respond to Dan McClellan's claim that Jesus was not God but a divine name bearer? | He rejects it as a critical-scholarship reading that pits texts against each other and does not persuade him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=13131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:51</a> | How does he feel about modern Gospel adaptations in media like The Chosen? | He says adaptations can be useful, but reading the Gospels is better and modern sensibilities can intrude when creators fill gaps. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=13712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:32</a> | Is a uniliteral Egyptian hieroglyphic text possible for biblical material? | He says he does not see why it would be needed when early alphabetic inscription systems already existed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=13922" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:02</a> | What was the purpose of uniting Amun and Re? | He says it consolidated sun-cult and royal-cult functions around the Theban dynasty and Karnak. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qQuVwgNpPk8?t=14025" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:45</a> | How and when was the Egyptian Book of Dreams codified? | He says the evidence is limited to Chester Beatty III, probably New Kingdom, and the exact codification history is unknown. |
